content.text: I found the streamable HTTP client and related tests; next I’ll inspect the client implementation and stale-session tests to understand the current 404 re-init behavior.

I found the streamable HTTP client transport config and the 404 transparent reinitialization path; I’m going to add a temporary regression assertion to reproduce the requested default-disabled behavior before changing the implementation.

input.description: Show final diff and working tree status

content.text:
Implemented the requested configuration gate for transparent streamable HTTP session re-initialization on 404.

Summary:
- Added `enable_reinitialization_on_404: bool` to `StreamableHttpClientTransportConfig`.
- Defaulted it to `false`, preserving safer behavior for stateful MCP servers.
- Added builder method:
  - `StreamableHttpClientTransportConfig::enable_reinitialization_on_404(bool)`
- Updated worker logic so transparent re-init/retry only happens when the config option is enabled.
- Updated the existing stale-session integration test to explicitly enable the option for the transparent re-init scenario.

Validation:
- Reproduced the issue by temporarily expecting default-disabled behavior; it failed before the fix.
- After the fix, ran:

```bash
cargo test -p rmcp --features client,server,transport-streamable-http-client-reqwest,transport-streamable-http-server --test test_streamable_http_stale_session
```

Result:
- `2 passed; 0 failed`

Notes:
- I did not commit changes.
- I did not leave temporary regression tests behind; the existing stale-session test was adjusted to account for the new explicit opt-in behavior.

result:
Implemented the requested configuration gate for transparent streamable HTTP session re-initialization on 404.

Summary:
- Added `enable_reinitialization_on_404: bool` to `StreamableHttpClientTransportConfig`.
- Defaulted it to `false`, preserving safer behavior for stateful MCP servers.
- Added builder method:
  - `StreamableHttpClientTransportConfig::enable_reinitialization_on_404(bool)`
- Updated worker logic so transparent re-init/retry only happens when the config option is enabled.
- Updated the existing stale-session integration test to explicitly enable the option for the transparent re-init scenario.

Validation:
- Reproduced the issue by temporarily expecting default-disabled behavior; it failed before the fix.
- After the fix, ran:

```bash
cargo test -p rmcp --features client,server,transport-streamable-http-client-reqwest,transport-streamable-http-server --test test_streamable_http_stale_session
```

Result:
- `2 passed; 0 failed`

Notes:
- I did not commit changes.
- I did not leave temporary regression tests behind; the existing stale-session test was adjusted to account for the new explicit opt-in behavior.
